transcript: Yes, I was lookin’ for Jacob.
What’d you say your name is?
Yeah! My name’s Whip. I was on a frigate recently and another shipman gave me this number to reach ya at. [delay] Yeah! My name’s Whip. I was on a frigate…
On a—On a ship, you were?—
—What are you guys haulin’ out there?
On a frigate. Yeah, uh-huh! He was a swabbie, you know? Ah, just a ship hand out there, sure—
—[laughs] Yeah! Sure! I know—I know exactly what you mean!

Yeah! Sure, sure! Anyway, I’m, ah, Admiral Whip Babbit; you can type that into your search engine, if you like. I’m currently on the afterbrow here—[delay] Babbit—and just wanted to [siren] say ahoy to ya a little bit!

Thank you! Got a couple things I wanted to touch base with ya on, ah, [siren] navigate with ya. I’m starting a neighborhood task force, ah, we’re gonna use to commission a new anchor. It’s gonna be the—like the Cadillac of anchors! [siren]—
—So I wanted to touch base with ya on that, see if you’re in a giving mood for something like that. [delay] It’s gonna be the Cadillac…
'Something drop forged, maybe, and, um, ah—
Yeah, w—it’ll come with a non-rolling horizontal winch—
—and it includes a custom baggie wrinkle, too; it’s gonna have everything!
[delay] It’ll come with a non-roll—So, are ya leaning as the crow flies? Are ya affirmative on that, er…
—‘Cause you’re the only one that I know that’s ashore right now; all my crewmen are adrift right now! [delay] …leaning as the crow “flies”? [siren]
[laughs] Right? The only real question I have is: are they biting?
Yeah, I could pick y’up in my barge here, bring ya back to my barracks. [exhales] You can help me batten down the hatch up here! [sniffs] [delay] Yeah, I could pick y’up in my barge…

Now, why don’t ya step into my brig here? ‘Cause, look: this isn’t some bumboat, okay? I’ve achieved total buoyancy out here where I’m at! I’m buooyant! [siren] [delay] ‘Cause, look: this isn’t some—and all my crew have got channel fever; they’re off on some dinghy somewhere or some donkey-mat. [siren]
Could you call me “Barnacle” or something like that?

Yeah, they’re on their dinghy, there. I’m a commodore and I need to hoist my daggerboard—that’s just what it comes down to—sunup, sundown! [siren] [inhales] What division are you in, anyhow? [exhales] [delay] sunup, sundown!
Uh! I mean, I’m a landlubber—
—primarily. So that—and I—as—as evidence of that, I did take a, uh, a—a catamarang [sic] of some kind, I think—
—and I became terribly seasick and I’m gonna get sick if I join ya—

W—well, I’ll tell ya, when we reach dry dock, you can go ‘head and secure the dunsel and the fife if you still have your gaff. [delay] I’ll tell ya, when we reach dry dock— And after that, we can, ah, go ‘head and hop on the gangplank [delay] —sel and the fife— I’ll remain on the helm! But we can, together, at least hitch the jackstaff or just swap out the jiggermast and lace the kickstrap just according to your own comfort level! ‘Cause, hey, Jacob, a—just on a serious thing, here: [delay] Just according to your own comfort level—

—Ah, I heard you had gone overboard on the mothership. [exhales] [delay] ‘Cause, hey, Jacob? Can I ask ya a—just on a serious thing, here— Now, I wanna tell ya, if that does occur—if that happens—just remember a few things, okay? [siren]

Ah, [delay] Now, I wanna tell ya, if that does occur—if that happens, just remem— to parbuckle your rope; forget all about containerization; maybe just piggyback off of a chandler—[delay] —buckle your rope— in his or her gantry; still, account for all wharfage and prepare for sheddage! [siren] But, you know, hey, privately, you can even, ah, tug on your thruster [siren] [delay] and prepare for sheddage!— Privately. If it’s entered into your Captain’s log, of course [siren] then I say, ah, stow your slop tank and say, “ahoy!” there, m’friend, huh? [delay] If it’s entered into your Captain’s log— [laughs] Cause—
—[laughs] [pitch-up & delay] I say, ah, stow your slop tank and say “ahoy!” there, m’friend, huh?—
—I’m intimid—I’m intimidated [siren] and the only knot I even know how to tie is a sheepshank—
—and I’m not even sure why I would tie it!

And, like I say, hey, between you and I: privately, you can even tug on your thruster! Ain’t no one ever died from it! [siren] I got another crewman here that maybe you can touch base with him, see if like where he’s comin’ from on the whole task force idea. Just ask him if you have any further questions about the anchor, okay?
Lemme hand you over to him. Come ‘ere, matey! Talk to Jacob—a helluva guy!
No [breaks up] Jacob, bud! I’m gettin’ some kinda talk on the phone here about gettin’ signed up to be on a—a boat.
Uh, and I’m—and I’ve never—I’ve hardly ever been on a boat but once and got seasick as all get-out!
Does that [laughs] Does that make any sense to you?
—Yeah, I don’t, ah—I’m p—I’m pretty con—I’m pretty confused about what’s happenin’ right now!

Well, I think there’s somebody out there who seems to take a great deal of pleasure in cross-connecting three people who, ah, just pick up their phones and say, “hello,” and they don’t know what the hell’s goin’ on and this guy seems to get a kick out of it! So, we just have to ignore the sonofabitch.
He’s got somebody on the third line and, ah, they don’t know it because they’re—they’re just picking up the sirens and stuff, so—
—just hang up and I’ll do the same and hopefully he’ll get tired of bothering us today but he’ll—he’ll be around again.
I said, “hopefully he’ll get tired of bothering us today and wait for another day.”
The guy who takes pleasure in screwing with the phone system. We haven’t been able to track him down, unfortunately.
Yeah. So have a good day! Stay safe!
What’s your first name? Who is your—who is this?
[breaking up] Hi, Mr. Trebek! I know who this is!
—a word you’re saying; you’ve got, ah, the background noise and you’re—
